description_long: This object is a compact, retro-styled electronic device with a trapezoidal body, primarily colored in olive green and brown. The front panel features a central analog dial labeled 'Search', flanked by two rotary knobs marked 'Volume' and 'Tune', and a prominent red button labeled 'On/Off' beneath the dial. The side panels each have three raised, rounded rectangular elements, possibly for ventilation or as part of the device's design. The back panel is plain and unadorned, suggesting that all user interaction occurs from the front. The casing appears to be made of metal or hard plastic, with a slightly worn, matte finish. This device could plausibly serve as a vintage radio, signal scanner, or specialized control box in an urban environment.
description: A compact, retro electronic device with an analog dial, two rotary knobs, and a red button on its olive green and brown trapezoidal body. The design suggests a vintage radio or signal scanner.
description_view_0: Front view showing the analog dial, two rotary knobs, and a red on/off button, with all controls and labels visible.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying three raised, rounded rectangular features and the side profile of the knobs.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain, unadorned olive green panel with no visible controls or features.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with three raised, rounded rectangular features and the side profile of the knobs.
